walkthrough.title: Choose and Assemble the Modular Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Clank! In! Space! set up. First, grab the command module board and place it in the center of the table. This is the starting area where all players begin. Then pick two of the double-sided module boards and connect them to the command module. The modules lock together with connectors on the edges. For your first game, use the sides marked with a one to keep things simpler.

walkthrough.title: Place Artifacts on the Board
walkthrough.text: Now find the artifact tokens. These are numbered point tokens that you will be stealing from Lord Eradikus's ship. Place one artifact face down on each artifact space marked on the board. The artifacts deeper in the ship are worth more points, but they are harder to escape with. Every player must grab at least one artifact before heading to the escape pods.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Market Row
walkthrough.text: Find the adventure deck. This is the big deck of cards you will be buying during the game. Give it a good shuffle and place it face down near the board. Flip six cards face up next to it to form the market row. Also set out the three reserve piles nearby. These are the always-available cards that never run out: Boldly Go, Memory Core, and Fazerblade.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Dragon Bag
walkthrough.text: Now let's set up the dragon bag. This black cloth bag is the heart of the Clank mechanic. Drop all twenty four black cubes into the bag. These are neutral cubes that act as padding. No colored cubes go in yet. The bag will get more dangerous as players add their Clank cubes throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Boss Track
walkthrough.text: Find the boss track on the side of the command module board. This is where you track Lord Eradikus's rage level. Place the rage marker on the starting space. As the game progresses, certain cards and events will advance the rage marker, triggering boss attacks that draw cubes from the bag.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Player Components
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a color and takes the matching thirty Clank cubes and one pawn. Place your pawn on the start space in the command module. You will also need a way to track your health. Each player starts with ten health points. Set aside any damage tokens near the board for easy access.

walkthrough.title: Build Starting Decks
walkthrough.text: Each player builds their starting deck of ten cards. Everyone gets the same cards: six basic Hack cards that provide one Skill each, two Stumble cards that generate Clank with no benefit, and two Scramble cards that provide movement and other basic abilities. Shuffle your ten cards together into a personal face-down draw pile.

walkthrough.title: Place Bounty Hunters and Secrets
walkthrough.text: Place bounty hunter tokens on their marked spaces on the board. These are enemies you can fight for rewards using Swords. Then scatter the secret tokens face down on their marked spaces throughout the ship. Secrets give you small bonuses when you pick them up, like extra points, healing, or free cards.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Data Port Tokens
walkthrough.text: Find the data port tokens and place them on the marked data port spaces across the board. Every player must hack at least two data ports before they can use an escape pod. Data ports are scattered throughout the ship, so plan your route to hit them along the way.

walkthrough.title: Draw Starting Hands
walkthrough.text: Each player draws five cards from the top of their personal deck. This is your starting hand for turn one. Keep your hand hidden from other players. Remember, you will draw back up to five cards at the end of every turn, reshuffling your discard pile when your draw pile runs out.

walkthrough.title: Determine First Player
walkthrough.text: Pick a starting player however you like. The player who most recently watched a science fiction movie is a fun choice. Play proceeds clockwise from the starting player. Everyone is ready to begin the heist on Lord Eradikus's ship. Let's learn the rules!

walkthrough.title: Goal of the Game
walkthrough.text: Let's learn how to play Clank! In! Space! You are thieves sneaking through Lord Eradikus's spaceship. Your goal is to steal an artifact, hack at least two data ports, and escape through an escape pod before the boss knocks you out. The player with the most victory points from artifacts, secrets, purchased cards, and credits wins the game.

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you play all five cards from your hand. Each card generates some combination of four resources: Skill, Swords, Boots, and Clank. You can use these resources in any order during your turn. After spending your resources, discard all played cards and any cards you bought. Then draw five new cards from your personal draw pile.

walkthrough.title: Skill: Buying New Cards
walkthrough.text: Each card in the market has a cost shown in the upper left corner. Spend Skill equal to the cost to buy the card and add it to your discard pile. You can buy multiple cards per turn if you have enough Skill. When you buy a card from the market row, immediately replace it with a new card from the adventure deck. You can also buy from the three reserve piles at any time.

walkthrough.title: Swords: Fighting Enemies
walkthrough.text: Some cards in the market row are enemy cards with a red banner. To defeat an enemy, spend Swords equal to its strength, then gain the listed reward. Defeated enemies go to a discard area, not your deck. You can also use Swords to fight bounty hunters on the board when you share a space with them. Defeating bounty hunters earns you rewards like credits, cards, or health.

walkthrough.title: Boots: Moving Through the Ship
walkthrough.text: Each connection between spaces costs one Boot. Some connections have special requirements, like a Sword icon meaning you must also spend a Sword to pass through. You can move as many spaces as you have Boots to spend. Plan your route carefully. You need to visit data ports, grab an artifact, and reach an escape pod.

walkthrough.title: Clank: Making Noise
walkthrough.text: When a card generates Clank, take that many cubes of your color from your personal supply and place them in the Clank area on the board. These cubes are not in the bag yet. They wait in the Clank area until a boss attack dumps them all into the dragon bag. The more cubes you have in the bag, the more likely you are to take damage.

walkthrough.title: Boss Attacks
walkthrough.text: A boss attack happens whenever a card with the boss attack symbol appears in the market row, or when certain events trigger it. During a boss attack, first dump all cubes from the Clank area into the dragon bag. Then draw a number of cubes from the bag equal to the current rage level. Black cubes drawn are safe and go back to the bag later. If a cube of your color is drawn, you take one damage. When you have taken ten damage total, you are knocked out.

walkthrough.title: Data Ports and Hacking
walkthrough.text: Data ports are special spaces scattered across the ship. When you land on or pass through a data port space, you may hack it by spending the required Skill shown on that space. Each hack gives you a reward and flips the token to show it has been hacked. You must hack at least two data ports before you are allowed to use any escape pod. This is a mandatory requirement, so plan your route to include data ports.

walkthrough.title: Collecting Artifacts and Secrets
walkthrough.text: When you enter a space with an artifact token, you may pick it up. You can only carry one artifact at a time, though some game effects let you swap for a better one. Artifacts are worth victory points at the end of the game. Secrets are smaller tokens scattered across the ship. Pick them up when you enter their space. They give you immediate bonuses like credits, healing, extra cards, or end-game victory points.

walkthrough.title: Teleport Pads
walkthrough.text: Some spaces have teleport pad icons. When you land on a teleport pad, you may immediately teleport to any other teleport pad on the board for free, without spending additional Boots. This is a powerful way to move quickly across the ship. Teleporting does not cost anything extra, but you must reach the teleport pad normally first.

walkthrough.title: The Market Row and Reserve Cards
walkthrough.text: The market row always has six face-up cards from the adventure deck. When you buy a card or defeat an enemy, replace it immediately. Some market cards have special icons. Cards with a boss attack symbol trigger an attack when revealed. The three reserve piles are always available: Boldly Go provides Boots and Skill, Memory Core provides Skill, and Fazerblade provides Swords. Reserve piles never run out.

walkthrough.title: Escaping the Ship
walkthrough.text: To escape, you must meet three conditions. First, you must be carrying an artifact. Second, you must have hacked at least two data ports. Third, you must reach an escape pod space. When you land on an escape pod and meet all conditions, you escape the ship. You are safe and will score all your points at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Countdown After First Escape
walkthrough.text: When the first player escapes, things get intense. Lord Eradikus goes on high alert. At the start of each remaining turn, an additional boss attack triggers automatically. The rage level may also increase, meaning more cubes are drawn per attack. Players still on the ship need to hurry. Every turn they linger, the bag gets more deadly.

walkthrough.title: Getting Knocked Out
walkthrough.text: If you take ten total damage, you are knocked out. Where you get knocked out matters a lot. If you are knocked out anywhere in the escape pod area of the ship, you still score your points, though you lose some bonus points for not escaping cleanly. But if you are knocked out deep in the ship, away from the escape pod area, you score zero points for the entire game. Everything you collected is lost. Getting close to the exit before pushing your luck is critical.

walkthrough.title: Game End and Scoring
walkthrough.text: The game ends when all players have either escaped or been knocked out. Players who escaped or were knocked out in the escape pod area add up their points. You score victory points from your artifact, any secrets with point values, credits you collected, and point values printed on cards in your deck. The player with the highest total wins. In case of a tie, the player who escaped first wins.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Heist
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted game with three players: Red, Blue, and Green. We will play through several turns so you can see how the deck-building, movement, and Clank mechanics work together. One of our players will make some common mistakes along the way so you can learn from them. Let's go!

walkthrough.title: Starting Position
walkthrough.text: All three players have their pawns on the start space in the command module. Each player has shuffled their ten starting cards and drawn five. Remember, your starting deck has six Hack cards for Skill, two Stumble cards that just create Clank, and two Scramble cards for movement. The market row has six cards face up and the three reserve piles are ready.

walkthrough.title: Red's First Turn: Buy and Move
walkthrough.text: Red plays all five cards. She gets three Skill from her Hack cards, one Boot from a Scramble, and one Clank from a Stumble. Red spends two Skill to buy a card from the market that gives Swords, and uses her one Boot to move one space deeper into the ship. She places one red cube in the Clank area from the Stumble. Then she discards everything and draws five new cards.

walkthrough.title: Blue's First Turn: Focus on Movement
walkthrough.text: Blue plays his five cards and gets two Skill, two Boots, and one Clank. Blue decides to spend both Boots moving two spaces deeper into the ship toward a nearby data port. He spends his two Skill on a Memory Core from the reserve pile. One blue cube goes in the Clank area. Good start. Blue is heading toward the data ports early.

walkthrough.title: Green's First Turn: The Clank Mistake
walkthrough.text: Green plays his cards and sees a powerful card in the market that costs five Skill. He only has three Skill, so he cannot afford it. He buys a cheaper card instead, but does not pay attention to the Clank icons on the cards he played. Green generated three Clank this turn. That is three green cubes going into the Clank area on turn one. That is a lot of noise for so early in the game.

walkthrough.title: Why Clank Management Matters
walkthrough.text: Let's pause and talk about why Green's situation is risky. Those three green cubes are sitting in the Clank area waiting for the next boss attack. When the attack happens, they will all dump into the dragon bag. More of your cubes in the bag means a higher chance of drawing your color and taking damage. Red and Blue each have one cube in the area, but Green has three. Green needs to be more careful about which cards generate Clank.

walkthrough.title: A Boss Attack Triggers
walkthrough.text: On Red's next turn, she buys a card from the market and the replacement card has a boss attack symbol. Time for Lord Eradikus to strike! First, all cubes in the Clank area get dumped into the dragon bag. That is one red, one blue, and three green cubes joining the twenty four black cubes. Then we draw cubes equal to the rage level. Let's say three cubes are drawn: two black and one green. Green takes one damage already. Red and Blue are untouched.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Watch Your Clank
walkthrough.text: See how Green took early damage because of all those cubes in the bag? In the early game, try to minimize Clank. Buy cards that give you resources without generating noise. Those Stumble cards in your starting deck are the worst offenders. All they do is create Clank with no benefit. When you get the chance, look for cards that let you trash Stumble cards to thin your deck.

walkthrough.title: Blue Hacks a Data Port
walkthrough.text: Blue has been moving steadily and reaches a data port space. He plays his cards this turn and has enough Skill to hack the port. He spends the Skill, flips the data port token, and collects the reward. That is one data port down, one more to go. Blue is making good progress toward his escape requirements.

walkthrough.title: Red Fights a Bounty Hunter
walkthrough.text: Red lands on a space with a bounty hunter. She has been buying Sword cards and now has three Swords to spend. The bounty hunter requires two Swords to defeat. Red spends two Swords, defeats the bounty hunter, and collects credits as a reward. She uses her remaining Sword to defeat an enemy in the market row for even more rewards. Swords are great for earning bonus resources.

walkthrough.title: Green Goes Deep: The Artifact Mistake
walkthrough.text: It is worth a lot of victory points, so Green goes for it. He spends several turns moving deeper and deeper, past the safer artifact spaces closer to the escape pods. Green grabs the big artifact, but now he is very far from any escape pod. He has also not hacked any data ports yet. This is a dangerous position.

walkthrough.title: Why Going Too Deep Is Dangerous
walkthrough.text: Green now faces a serious problem. He needs to hack two data ports and travel all the way back to an escape pod. That is a lot of Boots needed over many turns. Every turn he spends traveling is another turn where boss attacks can deal damage. If Green gets knocked out this deep in the ship, he scores zero points. That shiny artifact will be worthless. The safer play is to grab a mid-value artifact closer to the escape route.

walkthrough.title: Blue Hacks Data Port Number Two
walkthrough.text: Blue has planned well. He reaches his second data port and hacks it. That is both data ports done. Now Blue just needs to grab an artifact and reach an escape pod. Blue picks up a nearby artifact worth a moderate amount of points. It is not the biggest artifact on the board, but Blue is in great position to escape safely.

walkthrough.title: Red Uses a Teleport Pad
walkthrough.text: Red lands on a teleport pad and uses it to zip across the ship to another teleport pad near a data port. This saves her several turns of movement. Red hacks the data port on her next turn. Teleport pads are extremely valuable for efficient movement. Always keep an eye on where they are on the board.

walkthrough.title: Green's Data Port Problem
walkthrough.text: Green finally realizes he needs two data port hacks before he can escape. He has been so focused on getting the big artifact that he skipped every data port along the way. Now he has to backtrack through dangerous territory to find data ports, spending precious turns while boss attacks keep coming. This could have been avoided by hacking data ports on the way in.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Hack Data Ports Along the Way
walkthrough.text: The lesson here is simple. Always hack data ports as you travel through the ship. Do not save them for later. If you push deep for an artifact without hacking ports, you will waste turns backtracking. Plan your route so that you pass through at least two data port spaces naturally. Treat data ports as mandatory waypoints, not optional detours.

walkthrough.title: Blue Escapes!
walkthrough.text: Blue has an artifact, two hacked data ports, and reaches an escape pod. Blue escapes the ship! He is safe and will score all his points. Now things get intense for Red and Green. Lord Eradikus goes on high alert. Extra boss attacks start triggering every turn. The clock is ticking.

walkthrough.title: The Countdown Pressure
walkthrough.text: With Blue out, Red and Green feel the pressure. Every turn now starts with an automatic boss attack before anyone plays cards. More cubes are drawn each time because the rage level increases. Green already has lots of cubes in the bag from earlier and is taking damage almost every attack. Red is in better shape but needs to hurry.

walkthrough.title: Red Escapes Safely
walkthrough.text: Red has hacked her two data ports, grabbed an artifact, and makes a beeline for the nearest escape pod. She arrives just in time, escaping with six health remaining. Smart play from Red throughout. She balanced buying cards, fighting enemies, managing Clank, and planning her escape route.

walkthrough.title: Green's Fate
walkthrough.text: Green is still deep in the ship, desperately trying to reach a data port. Another boss attack fires. Four cubes are drawn and two are green. Green takes two more damage, bringing him to nine out of ten. One more hit and he is knocked out in the depths of the ship. On his next turn, Green finally hacks a data port, but the next boss attack draws another green cube. Green is knocked out with only one data port hacked, deep in the ship. Green scores zero points.

walkthrough.title: Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: Let's add up the scores. Blue escaped with a moderate artifact, several secrets, credits, and some high-value cards. Blue scores around forty five points. Red escaped with a decent artifact, some enemy rewards, and a well-built deck. Red scores around forty points. Green scores zero because he was knocked out deep in the ship. All that effort for nothing. Green's mistakes cost him the entire game.

walkthrough.title: Before You Launch
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your own game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to card types, resources, and game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

summary.title: Before You Launch
bullets: Check out General Tips → strategy basics for beginners
Reference the Appendix → quick-reference for terms and mechanics
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we talk strategy, here are the biggest mistakes new players make. First, ignoring data ports until it is too late and then needing to backtrack. Second, diving deep into the ship for a big artifact without enough movement to escape. Third, generating too much Clank early and flooding the bag with your cubes. Fourth, buying expensive cards that will not cycle into your hand before the game ends. Keep these in mind and you will avoid the biggest beginner traps.

walkthrough.title: Plan Your Route Early
walkthrough.text: As soon as the board is assembled, scan for three things: data port locations, artifact locations, and escape pod positions. Plan a rough route that hits at least two data ports, passes by a good artifact, and ends at an escape pod. You do not need to lock in every step, but having a general path in mind prevents wasted movement. Teleport pads can act as shortcuts, so factor them into your route planning.

walkthrough.title: Manage Your Clank
walkthrough.text: Clank management is the difference between winning and scoring zero. Every cube of yours in the dragon bag is a chance to take damage. In the early game, minimize Clank whenever possible. Avoid buying cards that generate Clank unless the reward is very strong. Your two starting Stumble cards are pure liabilities. They generate Clank with no benefit, so look for cards that let you trash them. As the game goes on, some Clank is unavoidable, but keeping your count low gives you more breathing room.

walkthrough.title: Deck Efficiency Over Deck Size
walkthrough.text: Do not buy cards just because you can afford them. Every card you add to your deck dilutes your draws. If you buy a card that does not directly help your strategy, it takes up space in your hand on future turns. Focus on cards that give you what you need: Boots for movement, Swords for enemies along your route, and Skill for key purchases and data port hacking. Trashing weak cards like Stumble is often better than buying new ones.

walkthrough.title: Know When to Turn Back
walkthrough.text: The hardest decision in Clank! In! Space! is knowing when to stop pushing deeper and start heading for the exit. A good rule of thumb is to grab an artifact when you have at least half your health remaining. If you have fewer than five health points, you should be heading for an escape pod immediately. The boss attacks accelerate after the first escape, so do not assume you have many turns left. A twenty point artifact that you escape with beats a thirty point artifact that gets you knocked out.

walkthrough.title: Use Reserve Piles Wisely
walkthrough.text: The three reserve piles are always available and often overlooked. Boldly Go is excellent early because it gives both Boots and Skill, helping you move and hack data ports. Memory Core provides reliable Skill for buying better cards later. Fazerblade gives Swords for dealing with enemies. Do not sleep on these just because they are not flashy market cards. Consistent, reliable cards win games more often than one splashy purchase.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Other Players
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to what your opponents are doing. If someone is heading for the same artifact you want, you might need to adjust your plan. If another player is close to escaping, the countdown is about to start, so you should be wrapping up your deep run. If someone has very few cubes in the bag, they can afford to take more risks than you. Use this information to time your escape. Being the second player to escape is often the sweet spot, as you benefit from a full game without triggering the countdown yourself.

walkthrough.title: Balance Risk and Reward
walkthrough.text: Every decision in Clank! In! Space! involves a risk-reward calculation. Bigger artifacts are worth more points but require more turns deep in the ship. Cards with powerful effects often generate Clank. Fighting enemies costs Swords but gives great rewards. The key is to take calculated risks, not reckless ones. Ask yourself on every turn: if a boss attack happens right now, am I in trouble? If the answer is yes, it is time to start heading for safety.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't ignore data ports — backtracking wastes critical turns
Don't dive too deep without enough Boots to escape
Minimize early Clank — flooding the bag kills you fast
Don't buy expensive cards that won't cycle before game ends

summary.title: Plan Your Route Early
bullets: Scan board: data ports, artifacts, escape pods, teleport pads
Plan a rough route: 2+ data ports → artifact → escape pod
Teleport pads are shortcuts — factor them in
Don't lock in every step, but have a general direction

summary.title: Manage Your Clank
bullets: Every cube in the bag = chance of damage
Minimize Clank early; avoid cards that generate noise without reward
Stumble cards are pure liability — trash them ASAP
Some Clank is unavoidable late game; keep count low early

summary.title: Deck Efficiency Over Deck Size
bullets: Don't buy cards just because you can afford them
Every card added dilutes future draws
Focus on Boots, Swords, and Skill that match your plan
Trashing weak cards often better than buying new ones

summary.title: Know When to Turn Back
bullets: Grab artifact when you have 5+ health remaining
Below 5 health → head for escape pod immediately
Boss attacks accelerate after first escape
20 VP artifact escaped > 30 VP artifact knocked out

summary.title: Use Reserve Piles Wisely
bullets: Boldly Go: Boots + Skill — great for early movement and hacking
Memory Core: reliable Skill for bigger purchases
Fazerblade: Swords for enemies along your route
Consistent cards win games over splashy one-offs

summary.title: Watch the Other Players
bullets: If someone is near escaping, countdown is about to start
Adjust if opponents target the same artifact
Track who has lots of cubes in bag — they're more vulnerable
Being 2nd to escape is often the sweet spot

summary.title: Balance Risk and Reward
bullets: Bigger artifacts = more VP but more turns deep in ship
Powerful cards often generate Clank — weigh the trade-off
Ask: 'If a boss attack happens now, am I in trouble?'
Calculated risks, not reckless ones

walkthrough.title: Speed Run Strategy
walkthrough.text: The speed run is a high-floor strategy that prioritizes escaping quickly with a moderate artifact. The idea is to buy cheap Boot cards and Boldly Go reserves early, hack your two data ports on the way in, grab the nearest decent artifact, and rush to an escape pod. You sacrifice the biggest artifact for consistency and safety. By escaping first, you trigger the countdown and put pressure on everyone else. This strategy wins through reliability rather than big plays.

walkthrough.title: Deep Dive Strategy
walkthrough.text: The deep dive goes for the highest-value artifacts at the back of the ship. This is riskier but can yield the highest scores. To make it work, you need a very efficient deck with lots of Boots and minimal Clank generation. Buy cards that give multiple Boots per card, and aggressively trash your Stumble cards. Hack both data ports on the way in so you are ready to escape as soon as you grab the artifact. The key is to be moving toward the exit before anyone else escapes and triggers the countdown.

walkthrough.title: Combat Heavy Strategy
walkthrough.text: This strategy focuses on Sword cards and fighting every enemy you encounter. Bounty hunters give excellent rewards including credits, healing, and sometimes cards. Market enemies also give great payoffs when defeated. By stacking Swords, you earn extra resources that other players miss. The combat strategy pairs well with a moderate-depth run because the rewards from fighting offset the cost of not rushing. Look for cards that give both Swords and another resource to maximize efficiency.

walkthrough.title: Deck Thinning and Card Quality
walkthrough.text: One of the most powerful advanced techniques is deck thinning. Your starting deck has two Stumble cards and six basic Hack cards. As you buy better cards, those weak starters dilute your draws. Cards that let you trash other cards are extremely valuable because removing one weak card improves every future draw. A lean deck of eight strong cards is far better than a bloated deck of fifteen mediocre ones. Prioritize trashing Stumbles first, then basic Hacks as you acquire better Skill sources.

walkthrough.title: Teleport Pad Routing
walkthrough.text: Mastering teleport pad routing separates good players from great ones. Before you start moving, map out where every teleport pad connects. A route that uses two teleport jumps can reach deep artifacts in half the turns of walking. The best routes hit a data port, teleport to another section, hack a second data port, grab the artifact, then teleport back toward an escape pod. Experienced players choose their board modules specifically for favorable teleport pad placement.

walkthrough.title: Clank Timing
walkthrough.text: Advanced players think of Clank not as something to always avoid, but as a resource to manage strategically. In the early game, minimize Clank because the bag has few cubes and yours will be drawn frequently. In the mid-game, generating Clank is acceptable if the card you buy is powerful enough. In the late game, if you are near an escape pod, generating a lot of Clank is fine because you will be gone before the next attack. Time your noisiest turns for when you are about to escape.

walkthrough.title: Secret Token Optimization
walkthrough.text: Secrets are easy to overlook, but they add up significantly. Some secrets give immediate healing, which directly extends your survivability. Others give credits or extra cards. Point-value secrets are pure victory points with no risk. Plan your route to pick up secrets along the way rather than making dedicated trips for them. A route that hits three secrets on the way to an artifact is worth much more than a slightly shorter path that skips them all.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Countdown
walkthrough.text: Knowing when the countdown is about to start is crucial for timing your escape. Watch who is closest to meeting all three escape conditions. If someone has an artifact, two hacked data ports, and is near an escape pod, the countdown could start next turn. When the countdown begins, the game accelerates dramatically. Plan to be within two or three turns of an escape pod when it triggers. Players who are caught five or more turns away from safety almost always get knocked out.

summary.title: Speed Run Strategy
bullets: Buy cheap Boot cards + Boldly Go reserves early
Hack 2 data ports on the way in, grab nearest decent artifact
Escape first → trigger countdown, pressure opponents
Wins through reliability, not big plays

summary.title: Deep Dive Strategy
bullets: Target highest-value artifacts at the back of the ship
Need efficient deck: lots of Boots, minimal Clank
Trash Stumbles aggressively; hack data ports on the way in
Start heading to exit BEFORE anyone else triggers countdown

summary.title: Combat Heavy Strategy
bullets: Stack Sword cards; fight bounty hunters + market enemies
Combat rewards: credits, healing, cards — big value
Pairs well with moderate-depth run
Look for cards that give Swords + another resource

summary.title: Deck Thinning and Card Quality
bullets: Trash weak cards (Stumbles first, then basic Hacks)
Lean 8-card deck >> bloated 15-card deck
Every card removed improves all future draws
Trash effects are among the most powerful in the game

summary.title: Teleport Pad Routing
bullets: Map all teleport connections before moving
2 teleport jumps can halve travel time to deep artifacts
Best routes: data port → teleport → data port → artifact → teleport → escape
Choose board modules for favorable teleport placement

summary.title: Clank Timing
bullets: Early game: minimize Clank (few cubes in bag = yours drawn often)
Mid-game: acceptable if card bought is powerful enough
Late game near escape: Clank doesn't matter if you're leaving
Time noisiest turns for when you're about to escape

summary.title: Secret Token Optimization
bullets: Healing secrets extend survivability directly
Point-value secrets = pure VP with no risk
Plan route to hit secrets along the way, not as side trips
3 secrets on your path >> slightly shorter path with 0 secrets

summary.title: Reading the Countdown
bullets: Watch who's closest to meeting all 3 escape conditions
Plan to be 2-3 turns from escape pod when countdown starts
5+ turns from safety when countdown hits = almost always knocked out
Time your escape — don't get caught deep when someone bolts

entries.definition: The currency resource used to buy cards from the market row or reserve piles, and to hack data ports. Generated by Hack cards in your starting deck and many purchased cards. Shown as a blue diamond icon.
The combat resource used to fight enemies in the market row and bounty hunters on the board. Some connections between spaces require a Sword to pass through. Shown as a red sword icon.

The movement resource. Each Boot moves your pawn one connection on the board. You need Boots to navigate the ship, reach data ports, grab artifacts, and get to escape pods. Shown as a yellow boot icon.

Noise you make while sneaking through the ship. When a card generates Clank, move that many cubes of your color from your personal supply to the Clank area on the board. During boss attacks, these cubes are dumped into the dragon bag, increasing your chance of taking damage.
Victory points earned from defeating bounty hunters, collecting secrets, and some card effects. Credits count toward your final score. Tracked with credit tokens.
A designated space on the board where Clank cubes accumulate before a boss attack. Cubes sit here until a boss attack triggers, at which point they are all dumped into the dragon bag together.

The black cloth bag from which cubes are drawn during boss attacks. Contains 24 black neutral cubes at the start plus any player cubes dumped from the Clank area. Drawing your color means you take one damage.

The 24 neutral cubes that start in the dragon bag. When drawn during a boss attack, black cubes cause no damage to anyone. They act as padding, making the bag safer. As more player cubes enter the bag, the proportion of safe black cubes decreases.

An event triggered by certain market card reveals or the countdown. First, all cubes in the Clank area are dumped into the dragon bag. Then cubes are drawn equal to the current rage level. Player-colored cubes cause one damage to that player. Black cubes are safe.

The boss of the spaceship. He does not appear on the board but attacks indirectly through the dragon bag mechanic. His rage level determines how many cubes are drawn per attack. After the first player escapes, his attacks become more frequent and severe.

A track on the board that determines how many cubes are drawn from the dragon bag during each boss attack. The rage level increases as the game progresses and escalates further after the first player escapes.

An icon on some adventure cards. When a card with this symbol is revealed to refill the market row, a boss attack immediately triggers. This makes buying and defeating market cards risky because you never know what the replacement card will be.

Special spaces on the board that players must hack by spending Skill. Hacking a data port gives a reward and flips the token. Players must hack at least two data ports before they are allowed to use any escape pod. Data ports are mandatory waypoints for escape.

The act of spending Skill at a data port space to flip the data port token and collect a reward. Each data port can only be hacked once by one player. Hacking two data ports is a mandatory requirement for escape.

Spaces on the board where players can leave the ship. To use an escape pod, you must carry an artifact and have hacked at least two data ports. Escaping safely means you score all your victory points. The first escape triggers the countdown.

The section of the board near the escape pods. If you are knocked out in this area, you still score your points but may lose some bonus. This is much better than being knocked out deep in the ship, which scores zero.

Triggered when the first player escapes. During the countdown, an automatic boss attack happens at the start of each turn. The rage level may increase, meaning more cubes drawn per attack. Remaining players must hurry to escape or risk being knocked out.

Valuable tokens placed on artifact spaces throughout the ship. Players must collect at least one artifact to escape. Artifacts deeper in the ship are worth more victory points. Each player can carry only one artifact at a time, though swapping is sometimes possible.

Face-down tokens scattered on marked spaces across the board. When you enter a space with a secret, pick it up and reveal it. Secrets grant bonuses like credits, healing, extra cards, or end-game victory points.

Enemy tokens placed on specific board spaces. When you share a space with a bounty hunter, you may spend Swords equal to its strength to defeat it and gain rewards such as credits, healing, or cards. Bounty hunters remain on the board until defeated.

Special spaces on the board. When you land on a teleport pad, you may immediately teleport to any other teleport pad on the board for free, without spending additional Boots. Teleporting is a powerful way to traverse the ship quickly.
The central board piece where all players start. Contains the start space, the boss track, and the Clank area. The command module connects to the modular ship boards that change each game.

Clank! In! Space! uses a modular board made of the command module plus two double-sided ship modules. Different module combinations change the layout of data ports, artifacts, secrets, teleport pads, and connections, making each game unique.

Six face-up cards from the adventure deck available for purchase. When a card is bought or an enemy is defeated, it is immediately replaced from the adventure deck. If the replacement card has a boss attack symbol, an attack triggers.

Three always-available card piles that never run out: Boldly Go (Boots and Skill), Memory Core (Skill), and Fazerblade (Swords). These provide reliable, consistent resources and can be purchased alongside market row cards.

The main deck of cards from which the market row is filled. Contains a mix of ally cards, gear cards, and enemy cards. Enemy cards have a red banner and are defeated with Swords rather than purchased with Skill.

Each player's identical 10-card starting deck: 6 Hack cards (1 Skill each), 2 Stumble cards (1 Clank each, no benefit), and 2 Scramble cards (movement and basic abilities). Improving beyond these starting cards is the core of the deck-building mechanic.
A starting deck card that generates one Skill. You begin with six of these. They are basic but functional. As you acquire better Skill sources, consider trashing Hack cards to thin your deck.

A starting deck card that generates one Clank with no other benefit. You begin with two of these. They are pure liabilities that make your deck worse. Trashing Stumble cards should be a priority whenever the opportunity arises.

Cards in the market row with a red banner. These are not purchased with Skill but defeated with Swords. Spend Swords equal to the enemy's strength to gain its reward. Defeated enemies leave the game and do not enter your deck.

Each player starts with ten health points. You lose one health each time a cube of your color is drawn from the dragon bag during a boss attack. When you reach zero health, you are knocked out. Some secrets and combat rewards can restore health.

When a player takes their tenth damage, they are knocked out. If knocked out in the escape pod area, they still score points but may lose some bonuses. If knocked out deep in the ship, they score zero points for the entire game.

Permanently removing a card from your deck. Trashed cards go to a shared trash pile and are never reshuffled into your deck. Trashing weak cards like Stumble and basic Hacks improves your deck quality by increasing the concentration of strong cards.

The process of drawing through your entire deck and reshuffling your discard pile to form a new draw pile. In a lean deck, you see your best cards more frequently. Purchased cards enter your discard pile and will not be drawn until your next reshuffle.
